Conditions A to Z
Threadworms 
Threadworms (pinworms) are tiny worms in your poo. They're common in children and spread easily. You can treat them without seeing a GP. 
Signs of threadworms 
You can spot threadworms in your poo. They look like pieces of white thread.
You might also see them around your child's bottom (anus). The worms usually come out at night while your child is sleeping.
See what threadworms look like in poo
Credit: Vaio1488 https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0/deed.en
Other symptoms can include: extreme itching around the anus or vagina, particularly at night irritability and waking up during the night Less common signs of worms include: weight loss wetting the bed irritated skin around the anus 
Non-urgent advice: See a pharmacist if:
you think you or your child have threadworms 
Find a pharmacy 
Treatment for threadworms from a pharmacy 
You can buy a medicine called mebendazole for threadworms from pharmacies. This is usually a chewable tablet or liquid you swallow.
Treat everyone over 2 years old in your household, even if they do not have symptoms.
Tell the pharmacist if you need to treat a child under 2 years old, or if you're pregna
